notes: Broadcasting-satellite service (BSS) — Ku band satellite TV downlinks. Foxtel/Optus satellite broadcasts received by household dish antennas.
Ku band satellite TV — mixed BSS/FSS. AUS88: FSS transponders may also carry BSS transmissions in this range.
Ku band. Fixed-satellite + broadcasting-satellite. Satellite TV downlinks.
Standard frequency and time signal (20 kHz). Internationally coordinated precision time/frequency broadcasts.
AUS57: this band may be used by defence radiolocation on condition of no interference to other services.
ILS marker beacons (75 MHz). Per AUS25, subject to ICAO Annex 10.
AUS103: CSIRO Murchison Radio Astronomy Observatory (MWA operates 80-300 MHz, SKAO 70-350 MHz). Interference constraints apply in this range.
AUS24: Introduction of new broadcasting stations in 85-87.5 MHz requires special international agreements. TV services may be transferred to other bands.
ITU radionavigation overlay row (Region 2 only — does not apply in AU Region 3). AUS87: radio astronomy constraints (MWA range).
ILS (Instrument Landing System) and VOR navigation aids. Per AUS25, subject to ICAO Annex 10 standards.

ACARS (Aircraft Communications Addressing and Reporting System) primary VHF data link. Australian primary channels: 129.125 MHz and 130.025 MHz. Digital operational messaging protocol transmitting engine data, flight plans, weather updates, and ATC clearances via AM-modulated FSK. NOT voice aviation communications. Within the AERONAUTICAL MOBILE (R) allocation per ICAO standards. Classify as acars, not aviation_vhf.
AUS26: Broadcasting allocation in 137-144 MHz is temporary legacy — no new assignments, existing stations to be transferred. AUS103: radio astronomy (MRO/MWA) constraints.
AUS26: Broadcasting allocation is temporary legacy — no new assignments. AUS103 radio astronomy constraints.
AUS26: Broadcasting allocation is temporary legacy — no new assignments. AUS103 radio astronomy constraints.
AUS103: CSIRO Murchison Radio Astronomy Observatory (MRO/MWA/SKAO) radio frequency interference constraints apply in this band (80-300 MHz range).

AIS (Automatic Identification System) VHF maritime vessel tracking. AIS Channel 87B: 161.975 MHz, Channel 88B: 162.025 MHz. GMSK modulation at 9600 baud. Mandatory SOLAS broadcast from all commercial vessels over 300 gross tonnage. Unencrypted public safety broadcast — legal to receive passively. VHF MARITIME MOBILE service. IMPORTANT: This is NOT marine satellite or general marine VHF. Marine satellite operates at L-band (1621.35-1626.5 MHz), entirely different spectrum. Classify as ais.
AERONAUTICAL RADIONAVIGATION — Non-Directional Beacons (NDBs) for aircraft navigation. AUS49: subject to ICAO Annex 10. AUS68: underground mine comms also authorised.
AERONAUTICAL RADIONAVIGATION — NDBs for aircraft navigation. AUS49: ICAO Annex 10. AUS68: underground mine comms authorised.
AUS87: CSIRO radio astronomy observatory (MWA 80-300 MHz) constraints apply in this range. Fixed/mobile secondary to radio astronomy.
AUS100: designated for Australian Defence Force use. AUS103: radio astronomy (MRO/MWA) constraints. Frequency band used for military UHF satcomm and tactical comms.
ILS glideslope (UHF component, 328.6-335.4 MHz). Per AUS25, subject to ICAO Annex 10.
Standard frequency and time signal satellite (400.1 MHz). Precision time/frequency reference.
Meteorological satellite (space-to-Earth) and meteorological aids (radiosondes/weather balloons). AUS29 applies in offshore areas.
Earth-to-space met satellite uplinks and meteorological aids.
Earth-to-space met satellite uplinks and meteorological aids.
Land mobile / PMR. AUS98 harmonised government band: includes emergency services (police, fire, ambulance) frequencies. AUS29 radiolocation primary offshore.
International distress frequency. EPIRB and PLB (Emergency Position Indicating Radio Beacon / Personal Locator Beacon) transmissions at 406 MHz. Monitored by COSPAS-SARSAT satellite system.
Land mobile / PMR. AUS98 harmonised government emergency services band.
Land mobile / PMR. AUS98 harmonised government emergency services band.

AUS94: 420.8-421.2, 424.8-425.2, and 427.8-428.2 MHz are designated primarily for defence. AUS99: 420.8-421.2 MHz also allocated to amateur on secondary basis. AUS98: harmonised government emergency services band.
Land mobile / PMR. AUS98 harmonised government emergency services band (includes 457-458 and 467-468 MHz repeater pairs).
Land mobile / PMR. AUS98 harmonised government emergency services band. Also meteorological satellite (space-to-Earth) secondary.
AU UHF CB (Citizens Band) channels 1-40 sit at 476.425-477.400 MHz within this allocation. Licensed under ACMA class licence — no individual licence required for compliant handheld UHF CB radios.
Maritime mobile — MF distress and calling frequency zone (500 kHz international distress in this range).
UHF television broadcasting band. AUS104: designated primarily for broadcasting under Broadcasting Services Act 1992.
AM broadcasting (medium wave). Adjacent to main AU AM band. AUS50: subject to Regional Administrative LF/MF Broadcasting Conference provisions.
AUS50: AU AM broadcasting subject to Regional Administrative LF/MF Broadcasting Conference (Geneva 1975) provisions. AUS74: fixed service must not cause interference to broadcasting.
Mobile cellular broadband. 700 MHz band (AU Digital Dividend) — 4G/5G. AUS103 radio astronomy constraints near MRO.
Mobile cellular. 850 MHz band — 4G LTE in AU (Telstra, Optus). AUS29: radiolocation primary offshore. AUS101A: defence secondary use.
Already noted in mimir_band. AUS29: radiolocation is primary service in offshore areas. AUS101A: defence secondary use permitted.
AU ISM band per footnote AUS32: 918-926 MHz (centre 922 MHz) designated for ISM/LoRa. The LoRa AU915 channel plan uses 915-928 MHz.
Mobile cellular / fixed. Adjacent to AU ISM band. AUS101A: defence secondary. AUS29: radiolocation primary offshore.
Mobile cellular. 900 MHz band upper segment — used by cellular operators in AU.
ADS-B at 1090 MHz falls within this AERONAUTICAL RADIONAVIGATION allocation
GNSS (GPS L5, Galileo E5, GLONASS). AERONAUTICAL RADIONAVIGATION + RADIONAVIGATION-SATELLITE. AUS25: ICAO Annex 10. AUS64: defence spread-spectrum use permitted.
GNSS (GPS L2, GLONASS L2, Galileo E6). EARTH EXPLORATION-SATELLITE + RADIOLOCATION + RADIONAVIGATION-SATELLITE. AUS87: radio astronomy observatory constraints.
GNSS (GPS L2, GLONASS L2). RADIOLOCATION + RADIONAVIGATION-SATELLITE. AUS1A: intended for RNSS/defence shared use.

RADIO ASTRONOMY passive band — protected from interference globally. AUS87: CSIRO observatories at Narrabri, Parkes, Mopra, Hobart, and Ceduna operate sensitive receivers. AUS103: MRO/ASKAP constraints also apply.
GNSS (GPS L1 at 1575.42 MHz, Galileo E1, GLONASS L1). Primary GNSS downlink band. AERONAUTICAL RADIONAVIGATION + RADIONAVIGATION-SATELLITE. AUS25 and AUS87 apply.
Maritime mobile-satellite (space-to-Earth) and mobile-satellite. Inmarsat maritime satphone downlinks.
Meteorological satellite (space-to-Earth). NOAA HRPT high-resolution imagery downlinks operate in this range.
Meteorological satellite (space-to-Earth). NOAA HRPT downlink band.
Meteorological satellite (space-to-Earth). Includes NOAA APT/HRPT and Meteosat downlinks.
Meteorological satellite (space-to-Earth). NOAA/Meteosat downlink range.
Mobile cellular. 1800 MHz band (4G LTE Band 3) — widely used in AU by all major operators.
Mobile cellular. 2 GHz band — 3G/4G.
Mobile cellular. 2 GHz band.
Mobile cellular + mobile-satellite (Earth-to-space). 2 GHz IMT band.
Mobile cellular. 2 GHz band.
AUS106: frequency band 2103.406-2109.406 MHz used by space operation/research/earth-exploration services to support the Bilateration Ranging Transponder System near Alice Springs (23°45'S 133°52'E).
Maritime mobile HF working frequencies.
Mobile cellular. 2.1 GHz band — 3G UMTS/4G downlink.
Mobile cellular. 2.1 GHz band — 3G/4G downlink.
Maritime mobile HF.
Maritime mobile HF.
AUS106A: 2284.5-2290.5 MHz used by space operation services supporting the Bilateration Ranging Transponder System near Alice Springs. AUS87: CSIRO radio astronomy constraints.
Standard frequency and time signal (2500 kHz).
Mobile cellular. 2.5 GHz band (4G LTE Band 41/42) — used by some AU operators.
Standard frequency and time signal with space research component.
Standard frequency and time signal.
Mobile cellular. 2.5 GHz band.
Mobile cellular. 2.5 GHz band — 4G LTE. Broadcasting-satellite secondary.
Mobile cellular. 2.6 GHz band.
Mobile cellular. 2.6 GHz band upper edge.
Aeronautical mobile (R) and radionavigation — airborne systems including radio altimeters (4200-4400 MHz). Per AUS25 ICAO Annex 10.
Standard frequency and time signal (5000 kHz).
Aeronautical mobile-satellite (R) and radionavigation. Future aeronautical satcomm. AUS25 applies.
Standard frequency and time signal with space research component.
Aeronautical mobile-satellite (R) and radionavigation. AUS25 applies.
Aeronautical mobile (R) — VHF data link (VDL) Mode 2 and future broadband aeronautical comms. AUS25 applies.
